Mors dag. Jag kommer ihåg det som att jag fixade någonting för mamma på morgonen det året, men jag är faktiskt osäker. Vi skulle iväg tidigt och spela bortamatch. Vissa av lagen vi mötte spelade i Malmö, men flera av dem låg lite runtom. Vi behövde åka en bit för att komma dit.

Det var en riktigt skitig match. Gräset var så tovigt att bollen inte gick att rulla, vilket spelade till vår nackdel eftersom att vår spelidé var att hålla bollen på marken. Vi hade domaren emot oss från start, vilket inte var ovanligt vid bortamatcher. Inte sällan var det en storebror som dömde, och jag klandrar ingen storebror för att ha ett bias. Men det sög, eftersom att motståndarlaget kom undan med en hel del.

Vi fick hörna. Tro det eller inte, men jag var duktig på huvudet, min längd till trots. Jag fick följa med upp, och hade min vanliga position där jag skulle gå för bakre stolpen. Bollen kom in, flimrades runt lite och landade för mig i utkanten av straffområdet. Jag måttade, och sedan vet jag inte riktigt vad som hände. Jag låg ned med en smärta i foten som jag aldrig någonsin tidigare eller senare känt. Nu i efterhand så förstår jag det som att en motståndare stod med sin fot i rak vinkel på andra sidan bollen, så när jag träffat bollen så åkte foten rätt in i undersidan av hans fot.

De spelade vidare trots att jag låg i straffområdet. Deras målvakt gick förbi mig utan att ens slänga en blick. Till slut gick bollen ur spel, mina lagkamrater skällde ut domaren och mina tränare kom in för att hjälpa mig. Jag hoppade av på ett ben, kunde inte stödja på foten. Min stortå dunkade något enormt. Min tränare fick av mig skon, böjde lite på den och konstaterade att den inte var bruten. Han sa till mig att sätta på skon igen och prova om jag kunde gå. Jag kom inte i med foten i skon igen.

När jag kom hem så skulle vi ha ätit middag med mormor. Jag hade ont, men brydde mig inte jättemycket om det. Satt med tån högt. Mamma övertalade mig att ringa 1177, till slut gick jag med på det. Vi var hemma igen ett par timmar efter midnatt efter att ha varit på vårdcentral, akuten, röntgen, vårdcentral och sjukhuset.

Du vet leden som gör att du kan böja tårna? Den består av brosk och ledband. Mina ledband hade fått sig en ordentlig smäll, och brosket var krossat i den yttre leden och skadat i den inre. Det går inte att göra någonting åt den typen av skador, man tejpar det bara. Jag fick gå på kryckor och ha en stel sko i ett par veckor, och skulle inte kunna spela fotboll på ett par månader. En katastrof.

Det var i Maj, vi hade två matcher kvar på säsongen. Jag var tillbaka i träning i Oktober-November. Hann spela sista matchen på nästa säsong. I samma veva hade jag börjat på gymnasiet och fotbollslaget skulle splittras.

Det blev slutet på min fotbollskarriär. Sedan dess har jag provat för något lag, men inte riktigt känt att det varit värt ansträngningen. Istället har jag hoppat omkring mellan olika aktiviteter, bland annat testat medeldistanslöpning och BJJ. Nu klättring och löpning.

Det jag vill säga med allt det här är att det kan vara onödigt att låsa in sig i en idé av hur vi ska vara eftersom att saker förändras. Jag hade kanske fortsatt spela om min tå inte hade åkt åt helvete. Istället fick jag hitta andra saker att göra som gav mig samma kick och känsla av sammanhang. Hitta andra sätt att vara på. Det tror jag har varit nyttigt.

title: Patience.
subtitle: Patience.

paragraphs.text: Patience.
Patience.
Let go of all thoughts of insecurity. Of not being enough. Of not going to get there.
Trust in the process. In your ability. In yourself.
Be patient. It’ll come.

title: Allt för alla.
subtitle: Han skrev att jag inte skulle ta illa upp och så. Att det inte var personligt, att han inte menade något illa. Jag rotade mig inför vad…

paragraphs.text: Allt för alla.

Han skrev att jag inte skulle ta illa upp och så. Att det inte var personligt, att han inte menade något illa. Jag rotade mig inför vad han skulle skriva, tänkte att det nog skulle vara både personligt och att jag skulle komma att ta illa upp. Så blev det inte.
Jag har ganska nyligen skapat en väldigt nära elation med en kompis. Vi har gjort ett gäng saker ihop det senaste året vilket gjort att vi spenderat en hel del tid tillsammans.

Bland annat så har vi hållit i en två killmiddagar där vi bjudit in ett gäng killar på middag för att prata om maskulinitet, heteronormer och för att bara spendera tid med andra snubbar. För middagarna har vi också haft teman som sårbarhet och relationer, med medföljande frågor för samtal.

En av gångerna hade vi något i stil med sårbarhet som ämne, och en av frågorna var om vi hade någon att gå till för att prata med om jobbiga saker. Vi pratade om det i gruppen, och kom fram till att vi har olika personer till olika grejer, typ. Det var inget konstigt med det.

Ett par dagar efter träffen så fick jag ett sms från min vän, där han frågade om jag känner att jag kan prata med honom om allt. Det kan jag, vilket också blev svaret tillbaka. Och så skrev han att det känner han också. Men att jag inte skulle ta illa upp nu, men att jag inte var hans förstaval om det kommer till att prata om vissa grejer.

Då isade det till lite i mig. Inte av rädsla, ilska, avundsjuka som ofta har kommit tidigare. Många gånger har mitt mål varit att vara allt för människor runt mig, att alltid kunna vara där, oavsett vad. Att vara förstahandsvalet för dem, oavsett om det gäller en hockeyfinal eller om deras sjuka farmor.
Det går inte, kommer aldrig att göra. Inte att vara nummer ett för alla jag känner. Omöjligt.

Det känns okej, vilket var vad jag märkte när sms:et kom. Det känns helt ok att inte vara nummer ett på alla områden för alla. Oavsett så vet jag att vi är otroligt nära vänner. Men han ska nog inte komma till mig i första hand om han behöver tröst eller bara slappna av. Då ska han gå till någon som är bättre på de grejerna.
Ingen kan vara allt för alla. Men du kan vara något för någon.

title: om att blogga.
subtitle: Jag glömde att blogga igår. Inte på grund utav att jag hade en miljon andra grejer att göra, utan för att jag sköt på det till sista…

paragraphs.text: Om att blogga.

Jag glömde att blogga igår. Inte på grund utav att jag hade en miljon andra grejer att göra, utan för att jag sköt på det till sista sekund. I det bröt jag en streak på över 200 dagar där jag bloggat varje dag. Eller ja, skrivit en text varje dag. Över. Borta.
Och det är okej.

Det är lite mer än okej faktiskt. Det är ganska kul. Det ger mig möjlighet att fundera över vad det betyder för mig att blogga varje dag. Varför, över huvud taget om, det är viktigt för mig just nu. Det ger lite frihet i att inte ha 200 poster som ligger och trycker i ryggen längre, för det är nästan så det känns.
Nu finns det 200 texter. De ligger där. Jag har skrivit dem. Inte alla utav lust och för att det är kul, men de har skrivits utav mig.
Och idag publicerar jag en ny text.

title: Opportunity.
subtitle: There’s just soooooo much opportunity at hand right now. Whatever we want to do, it’s pretty much doable with a bit of effort and some…

paragraphs.text: Opportunity.

There’s just soooooo much opportunity at hand right now. Whatever we want to do, it’s pretty much doable with a bit of effort and some time. We can reach almost half of the population on this planet with our thumbs.

That’s kept me going for the past days. The gratitude of being able to live. Gratitude for being born in the wealthiest part of the world, at the best possible time to be alive. It’s just preposterous not to acknowledge that I’m better off then almost all of the people who’ve ever lived.

I caught myself having a conversation with a friend about this the other night. How many of our friends take things for granted, don’t really put in the work to put themselves in a position to succeed yet feeling entitled to succeed. How people around us in general seem to focus on what they don’t have. And we’ve got a bunch of people who’ve come here with nothing. With a wish to build a future, with ambition of building a life for themselves and their families.
Comfort is always going to lose. Always.
The difference between comfortable and grateful is that grateful doesn’t imply entitled. It doesn’t imply being at the end of the road. It’s movement forward, always keeping the past in mind.
Use gratitude instead of comfort.

title: On prio
subtitle: I’ve heard that priority is originally without a plural form. It’s priority, not priorities. It’s something prior to everything else…

paragraphs.text: On priority.
I’ve heard that priority is originally without a plural form. It’s priority, not priorities. It’s something prior to everything else. Something that is more important than everything else.
I’ve had a group of activities as a priority before, I haven’t had just one in a very long time. Until the past couple of days.

I’ve been working late nights with my bachelor thesis together with one of my closest friends. It’s been a really important experience, putting everything else on ice to get this done. Just as well as it’s been fun, it’s been kind of soothing. I’ve been able to relax every other thought and process. Some kind of rest in focus.

title: Late nighters.
subtitle: I’ve never really understood the value of being up late at night, especially for working. It’s just never been my thing.

paragraphs.text: Late nighters.
I’ve never really understood the value of being up late at night, especially for working. It’s just never been my thing.
An yet, I’ve done two late nighters in the past week with tremendous results. Together with a writing partner, I wrote over 8 pages on our bachelor thesis the other night, staying up until 3 am.
I understand the peace and quiet now. Just as I’ve had my early mornings, the nights are indescribably quiet.
I can finally enjoy the late nights.

title: Do wha
subtitle: One thing that I often tell people is to start with an egg. When you want to start something, a good habit or whatever, start out small…

paragraphs.text: Do what works.

One thing that I often tell people is to start with an egg. When you want to start something, a good habit or whatever, start out small. If you want to become more healthy, start by having an extra egg with your breakfast. Or to eat an egg for breakfast, if you don’t usually do so. Start small, to later expand.
That usually works for people. It usually gets people where they want to be in level of engagement towards their goal.
But.
Not for everybody.

I’ve recently started exercising pretty intensely compared to what I’ve been doing the past couple of years. What I’ve realised with myself ids that I can’t really start with an egg and take it from there. I need to go all in. I know this from other contexts. I need to dive, head first, in to whatever I take on.
Start with an egg. Or go all in. Or do something else. Do what works.
Get out through the door.
One of the best tips I’ve ever given is to get out through the door. Get out in to the world, but more importantly: get out of your own space. It’s been crucial for my productivity.

I’ve had days spent in my apartment that have been productive. I’ve had far more days spent in my own or someone else office that have been productive. I’ve also had far more days spent at home with the intention of being productive, which have turned out with me doing something completely else.

That’s why I’ve developed a habit of bugging out every day, to the extent that I can. Each day, I try to get out through the door fairly early, prepping my backpack with everything I might need throughout the day, and staying away from home until as late as necessary.

It gives me peace at home, as well as a focus at wherever I am at the moment. It’s soothing to know that I’m going to stay out until I get whatever I want to get done, done. And I actually tend to get it done if I’m not at home.
Get out through the door.

title: Decisions.
subtitle: I’ve had some kind of dip for the past days of not wanting to do any of the things that I’m currently pursuing. Not wanting to vlog, to…

paragraphs.text: Decisions.

I’ve had some kind of dip for the past days of not wanting to do any of the things that I’m currently pursuing. Not wanting to vlog, to record podcast episodes, to write texts or to go to meetings. Nothing.
In these situations, my decision box helps a lot. It makes me keep my promises to myself, and not fall to some kind of temptation to quit. Because I know that if I did, I’d regret it later on.
I want to make my decisions when I’ve got the appropriate resources to do so. When I’ve had time to weigh pros and cons against each other as well as to feel what my gut wants to tell me.
That’s why I keep going. Because at that time, I said I would.

title: On networking.
subtitle: I saw a video on networking today, by Gary Vaynerchuk, where he talks about networking for introverts. It points a finger to something…

paragraphs.text: On networking.

I saw a video on networking today, by Gary Vaynerchuk, where he talks about networking for introverts. It points a finger to something I’ve been experiencing for myself — mingles and networking events don’t really work for me and I’ve struggled to voice how my network has grown to the size it is.
A friend described it as when I enter a room full of people that I don’t know, I can introduce myself with the phrase “I’m Caspian and we’ve got common friends.”.

What Gary points out, and what I can relate to in that is how some of us just don’t want to be in crowds of people we don’t know, trying to get to know them. My biggest networking strategy has been to get to work with people. To have deep conversations in smaller groups. Because that’s where I’m comfortable, that’s where I can provide value.
That’s ultimately what you’ll have to think about. Where can you create the most value for someone else? In what situations can you get the most out of yourself, and possibly elevate someone else?

With that being said, I know that I’d like to be able to network in mingles and such as well. I know that my way of getting to know people might be an excuse not to try other ways. There’s always the “or not” factor.

title: Hyper focus.
subtitle: I’ve got an ability to hyper focus at things. It’s sort of like how we would see an autistic person obsess about something.

paragraphs.text: Hyper focus.
I’ve got an ability to hyper focus at things. It’s sort of like how we would see an autistic person obsess about something.

I realised this today as i downloaded a game for my iPad, and since have had a hard time to think about anything other than that. It’s a pain in this case, just as it might be a superpower at other times.
Be aware of this process, and make it work for you rather than against you.

title: Do you fear it or are you loving it?
subtitle: I’ve done a lot of things out of fear. Fear of failure, if being misunderstood, of being rejected. Fear in general.

paragraphs.text: Do you fear it or are you loving it?
I’ve done a lot of things out of fear. Fear of failure, if being misunderstood, of being rejected. Fear in general.
I’ve done some things out of love. All of them have turned out better. All of the actions followed by a feeling of love have been better off.
Are you doing it out of fear or out of love?

title: The trap of inspiration.
subtitle: I think inspiration can be extremely toxic. It’s a drug that we constantly get high on. Misused, we find ourselves always needing more and…

paragraphs.text: The trap of inspiration.

I think inspiration can be extremely toxic. It’s a drug that we constantly get high on. Misused, we find ourselves always needing more and bigger hits off it to actually get to action. In order to realise this, there needs to be some sort of self reflection to realise it’s happening.
Never justify creativity with inspiration. Never justify hard work with inspiration. Never justify anything with inspiration.

One of my closest friends kept saying he needed to smoke weed in order to create music. That weed got him going well above his creative capacity. What he manifests become true. He’s created an addiction for himself.
Luckily, inspiration neither as expensive nor as neurologically damaging to you as weed. Yet, as it might cripple your ambitions, it needs to be put in place. What place you might ask?
Inspiration needs to be followed by action. Action with an another reason then solely the source of inspiration.
Let me state that again.
Inspiration needs to be followed by action. Action with an another reason then solely the source of inspiration.

Don’t run because you got inspired by that fit girl on Instagram. Run because you want your body to be better off for the future. It’ll get you a lot further. With that being said, it’s totally fine to think of that instagram girl and run an extra intervall because of the thought.

The difference is the last little push. To manage that, we sometimes need something or someone to look up too. Sometimes. The trap of inspiration exists here as well, as soon as we get up to a level where we can actually manage the last intervall without the inspiration.

Don’t confuse inspiration with motivation. And be really really careful not to confuse inspiration with inner motivation. Because inner motivation is what’s really going to get you where you want to go.
Writers block.

I’ve been struggling with getting my head to a place where I feel comfortable writing lately. I don’t know what it is, but as soon as I sit down to write the blog post for the day, I keep getting distracted. It takes a lot more focus than usual to get the full text out.

I see this as an opportunity rather than an obstacle. It’s a challenge to write the text, a challenge I happily accept. All with the knowledge that I’m going to write, each and every day, because I said I would.

title: Balling out.
subtitle: I had a beer too much today. Or well, that’s a beer too much by my standards. Being a lightweight and alchohol newbie, that’s having a…

paragraphs.text: Balling out.
I had a beer too much today. Or well, that’s a beer too much by my standards. Being a lightweight and alchohol newbie, that’s having a second beer at the company of someone not having a second beer.

That to some degree significates something that may of my friends have been talking about, how milennials are too well behaved and orderly. How we don’t let loose in the same way as people used to. And most of my friends are more orderly than their parents were at their age. I most certainly am, having had a partying mother and a father addicted to drugs. And they had me at about their age.

The “worst” thing I’ve done is to break in at an aboneded building site at night. That’s also the only thing I can brag about. Many of my friends have none of those storys, while their parents have plenty.
Times are different. Balling out in my terms is having a whiskey after those two beers, by myself, at home. It might not have been for my mom. That’s just the way it is.

title: What you can’t change, you can change your attitude towards.
subtitle: There are some things you can’t change. Despite people saying that you can change everything, the truth is you really can’t. Not to our…

paragraphs.text: What you can’t change, you can change your attitude towards.

There are some things you can’t change. Despite people saying that you can change everything, the truth is you really can’t. Not to our knowledge at least. What you can change is probably a lot more than you think, but that’s another text.
You might not be able to change everything, but you can change your reaction to it.

In soccer, you can’t decide what tactics or even players your oponent is going to apply on field during your next game against them. You can decide to try to counter that tactic, or run by your own game. Those are your options.
That’s the case for all of these things that happen to us. We need to decide what type of person we want to be, and react in a way to become that person.

title: Why football?
subtitle: I’m on a 60 hour workshop marathon kind of thing hosted by one of my closest friends. We’ve been going at it for over 36 hours when I…

paragraphs.text: Why football?

I’m on a 60 hour workshop marathon kind of thing hosted by one of my closest friends. We’ve been going at it for over 36 hours when I exlaim “What the fuck, it’s 10pm. I’ve got a game!” tumbeling through the living room to get my computer.

Helena, the friend, asked me why. I realised that my usual explanation of “I’d really love it if I wouldn’t.” would not do well in this company. That’s how I realised that I’ve chosen football. I really really love this game and my team. Love watching the game and the feeling a win gives me.
It’s part of me, and I’ve chosen it over and over again.

title: Perspective.
subtitle: More and more I think all of our existense is about perspective. That’s what every conversation comes down to, it’s a matter of…

paragraphs.text: Perspective.
More and more I think all of our existense is about perspective. That’s what every conversation comes down to, it’s a matter of perspective.
Almost every bump in your road could be a problem. OR it could be an opportunity.
Almost evrything anyone says to you could be interpreted as rude. OR it’s a well ment compliment that’s really well hidden.
Almost everything you do could be really hard. OR it could be a learning experience.
It’s all about the interpretation.

title: I’m in some sort of rough patch right now, both mentally and physically.
subtitle: Today, I made a breakthrough with a conversation I had, where we discussed fear. Saying that there are two different ways to relate to…

paragraphs.text: The difference in acceptance and surrender.

I’m in some sort of rough patch right now, both mentally and physically. There’s a lot more that I’d like to do then I can. I’m feeling tired and overwhelmed almost all the time. I’m tending more to my negative thoughts then I’d really like to. And so on. It’s not been great to be in my head lately, to just put it that way.

Today, I made a breakthrough with a conversation I had, where we discussed fear. Saying that there are two different ways to relate to parts of us. Either we accept that they’re a part of us and surrender to it, or we accept it being a part of us, and make a conscious decision on how we want to handle it. In our conversation it was about how we handle the fear of our feelings. Either we say “I’m the sort of person who becomes aggressive when I’m angry” or we say “I’ve observed that I become aggressive when I’m angry. I’d like to change that.”.

I’m not surrendering to the rough patch. I don’t find myself a victim of it. I just accept that I’ll have to relocate my recourses for the time being, to handle things that would have been seamless otherwise. I’m making that decision.

title: Declutter.
subtitle: One of the most important things my mom have observed about me is how I need lists to function properly. At every point in time that I’ve…

paragraphs.text: Declutter.

One of the most important things my mom have observed about me is how I need lists to function properly. At every point in time that I’ve come to her with a cluttered mind, the response has been the same. “Write your lists.”
Yesterday, I had abreally tough time with my mind. It was going all over the place, giving me a hard time focusing. So much so that I felt incapable of writing my daily blog post. It was horrific.
Until I started writing my lists.
I’ve become really good at breaking things down because of the lists. Taking things that feel like a giant monster, breaking it up to harmless atoms.
That’s how I declutter my mind. How do you get yours straight?

title: Om smärta
subtitle: Bakom mig hördes ett brak. Först en duns, ett skrapljud, och sedan en större duns. Min uppmärksamhet var riktad mot en av de andra…

paragraphs.text: Om smärta.

Bakom mig hördes ett brak. Först en duns, ett skrapljud, och sedan en större duns. Min uppmärksamhet var riktad mot en av de andra bouldrarna som var aningen mer avancerad än oss. Jag vände mig om snabbt, och såg att han rullade runt på mattan som ska fånga oss klätterapor om vi ramlar.

Jag tog mig över på hans matta, och satte mig bredvid honom. Han ojade sig, och häll för ögonen och ena benet. Det gjorde uppenbart ont, och han grät nästan. Jag frågade vad som hade hänt, och la min hand på benet där han höll sig. Han hade tappat greppet om väggen. Förmodligen hade han inte hunnit skjuta ut sig från väggen innan och därför skrapat sig över benet. Otur.

Jag försökte få honom att sträcka ut benet, han sa att han inte kunde. Det såg inte ut som att någonting var brutet, så jag utgick från att det var ett “Jag tror inte att jag kan”, snarare än en sanning. Så vi fokuserade på att andas istället, för att lugna ner kroppen. In genom näsan. Ut genom munnen. Djupa andetag. Så. Klapp över smalbenet. In genom näsan. Ut genom munnen. Fortsätt andas.

När han låg där kom en man fram och frågade hur det var med honom. Jag blev förvånansvärt glad att han brydde sig, ingen av de andra som var i rummet verkade ha rört en min. Jag sa att det nog var ganska lugnt, att vi kollade så att allt var helt. Han log. Sa att vi skulle gå ut i receptionen om det var något. Jag tackade.

Han lugnade ned sig efter en liten stund. Andningen funkar väldigt bra på honom. Jag fick böja ut båda benen och lite med fötterna. Försäkrade mig om att inget var brutet. Berättade att det kommer göra ont. Skrapsåren i duschen och benet i ett par dagar. Blåmärken får man nog räkna med.

Efter ännu en liten stund så berättade jag att innan vi går så vill jag se honom på väggen en gång till, oavsett hur ont det gör. Han har ett par favoritproblem att lösa. Men en gång till behöver du klättra sa jag, för att du inte ska komma ihåg hela den här träningssessionen som negativ på grund utav en dålig runda. Han gick med på det. Han löste två problem till innan vi gick.

Det slog mig på bussen på väg hem hur lik mig och min lillebror han är. Hur ömtåliga vi är när vi inte är vana vid smärta. Jag kom ihåg hur jag hade inlinesskydd på mig på fotbollsträningarna när jag stor i mål. Till mitt försvar spelade vi på grus. Jag kom också ihåg min tränare som innan den första träningen i mitt nya lag skrattade och sa “De där behöver du inte. Kom nu.” Efter det behövde jag inga inlinesskydd. Jag lärde mig att hantera smärta och har otaliga gånger haft brännskador på benen, fyllda med svarta gummiploppar, ont i precis varje del av kroppen efter en tuff match eller en lång löprunda.

Smärta är naturligt för oss. Vi behöver sätt att hantera den på, och någon måste visa oss de sätten. Det är en del av varför vi har mentorer. Jag har på intet sätt besegrat min smärta, men konceptet av att min kropp klarar mer än jag tror ligger i bakhuvudet hela tiden. Bara jag tar ett djupt andetag.

title: It has to add rather then take.
subtitle: I’ve been a relentless advocate for social media, and still am. I’ve been talking a lot about the good things that social medias have…

paragraphs.text: It has to add rather than take.

I’ve been a relentless advocate for social media, and still am. I’ve been talking a lot about the good things that social medias have brought, especially in forums where the worse sides have been talked about to a larger extent. Yet, I’ve found myself sort of moving away from both Facebook and Instagram when I’ve deleted the apps and limited my time on the plattforms. Here’s why.
I find all of the good parts about social media so incredibly underrated. We can reach out to so many people on this planet from our palms, which has never been possible before. It’s truly amazing.

Something even more amazing in my eyes is the ability to create things. Stories. Videos. Small teachings. Value in all shapes or forms. Interactions. Purpose. Even physical things like a scarf or food. I find the ability to create more exhilarating than the idea to connect.

Simply because if we can’t create something out of our connection, if we’re just interacting to be interacting, it’s no good. It won’t make any difference, and it won’t be any interesting to me. Creating something has to come at first hand.

To me, social media plattforms can both help and hinder that process. It’s been both ways for me, as I create a lot of content to share, which is one of the drivers for me to create. Without the ability to connect over it, I might not have created it. I’ve also found myself in a scrolling tunnel, just consuming instead of creating. Not even bothering to consume, but just scroll.
In those cases, social media is used not to connect but rather as something to hang our attention to. It’s often an addiction, and we often times don’t realise.

That’s why I’ve taken a step back from the plattforms. I’ve realised that it might not be the best thing for my creative side to be on there as much as I’ve been. On the other hand, I also feel like I miss some parts of it. I’ve been peeping in on instagram a couple of times lately, and realised that I’ve missed the content of people like @babin and @drock a lot since I left. Their posts often times motivate me to create.

There’ll be no better punchline to. This text then this: too much of anything will hurt. To me, the single most important thing is creativity, in all its forms. Creativity can be increased and decreased by social media depending on the usage. I’m still trying to find that balance. Have you found yours?

title: Making your time count.
subtitle: Many of us have tons of book we’d like to read. Plenty of series to watch. Hours we’d like to spend with family and friends. Conserts we’d…

paragraphs.text: Making your time count.

Many of us have tons of book we’d like to read. Plenty of series to watch. Hours we’d like to spend with family and friends. Conserts we’d like to go to, things we’d like to do. Many of us have enough things for at least a couple of lifetimes.
I dare say almost all of us feel that way at times.
There’s one tool for you to master if you’d like to make your time count even more that it does now. Prioritising.

Being able to distinguish important from more imortant is crucial. To distinguish in a hurry from urgent is also crucial. Those skills are some that we need to practice, to level, in order to make our time count as much as it possibly could. It’s the single most important thing to learn.
What’s your priority right now?

title: Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag.
subtitle: Inga av mina bästa dagar har spenderats helt själv. Inga av mins bästa dagar har jag tittat på serier hela dagen. Inga av mina bästa dagar…

paragraphs.text: Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag.

Inga av mina bästa dagar har spenderats helt själv. Inga av mins bästa dagar har jag tittat på serier hela dagen. Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag scrollat timmar på Facebook. Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag spenderat med människor jag inte tycker om. Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag suttit still hela dagen. Inga av mina bästa dagar har jag konsumerat mer än jag producerat. Inga.

title: I fucked up.
subtitle: I’ve been late on a number of things for two days, all part of my red line. All of them very important to me. None of them stress me…

paragraphs.text: I fucked up.
I’ve been late on a number of things for two days, all part of my red line. All of them very important to me. None of them stress me nearly as much as I’d thought they would.

I missed my vlog yesterday. Not recording it, but publishing it. I was super close on missing my blog text yesterday, and just not doing it today. I missed the upload of my podcast episode which was supposed to be up last night. And so on.
And it’s all okay.

They’re all deadlines for myself. They’re all deadlines put in place because I know that I feel good when I do these things. These two days, I just couldn’t get things done in a regular manner. I trust that if I could, I would.

It’s not about letting things slide with myself. It’s about trusting that when everything comes around, I’ve put things in a place where making the long term best option will be easier that the others.
Set ambitions. Get your red line as far up as you can. But remember to not beat yourself up when you don’t get there. Asess why you didn’t, and try again tomorrow. Adjust. Be practical.

title: We’re tough.
subtitle: I’ve had two relatively crazy days, with very little sleep and strange encounters. I’ve slept a total of three hours, never more than an…

paragraphs.text: We’re tough.

I’ve had two relatively crazy days, with very little sleep and strange encounters. I’ve slept a total of three hours, never more than an hour at a time, in the past 42 hours. It’s had it’s effects on my psyche and body.

What I’ve gathered from all of this is that our bodies are extremely adaptable. These are in no ways extreme conditions, yet they’re pushing my limits a bit. It’s strange to see that I can do without sleep in this way.
Yet again a sign of mind over matter in my book.

title: If not the stories.
subtitle: I got myself some tankespjärn toat at a very unexpected dinner. I got to eat food with Dubfire, a world famous DJ, his manager and his…

paragraphs.text: If not the stories.

I got myself some tankespjärn toat at a very unexpected dinner. I got to eat food with Dubfire, a world famous DJ, his manager and his friend. The friend, particularly, got talking about crazy nights out, with crazy ammounts of alcohol, among other things.

And as have happened before, it dawned on me how far from that I am in life. How few crazy nights out I’ve got to talk about. (That’s none really.) And how much I might have missed out on due to that.
Not only talking about craziness, but the whole experience of not having proper control and so on. I think there’s something there, if not the stories.

title: Don’t be fooled in to believing creativity is some magic kind of superpower that some people were…
subtitle: Creativity is the ability to combine things. That might be physical things, concepts, thoughts or tones. Anything really. In order to do…

paragraphs.text: There’s creativity in everyone. Find it.

Don’t be fooled in to believing creativity is some magic kind of superpower that some people were born with. We’ve all got some preconditions to what we might excel at. Some people have higher intelligence, some are taller, and some have nails that are just perfect for peeling oranges. None of the above necessarily means that the person is creative.

Creativity is the ability to combine things. That might be physical things, concepts, thoughts or tones. Anything really. In order to do that, we need to take in elements to combine. That’s the key to creativity.

Some people are better at taking elements in. Some people are better at remembering things. Some people have more energy to read, listen and perceive. But most importantly, the creative people have practiced taking elements in for a long time.
Practice taking elements in, and the combinations will follow.

There are an unlimited amount of elements to take in. Angles on buildings, whole books, movies and documentaries not to mention all of the conversations you can have. Try adding a new one, something seemingly unconnected to what you usually do. I promise you, there’ll be a connection. You just need to find it.
There’s creativity everywhere, in everyone. Find it.

title: A night of belonging.
subtitle: I’ve had a habit of inviting people that I find interesting to my home for quite some time. Both for podcast conversations, coffee and…

paragraphs.text: A night of belonging.

I’ve had a habit of inviting people that I find interesting to my home for quite some time. Both for podcast conversations, coffee and dinner. Sometimes by ourselves, sometimes a bunch of us. It’s built some kind of tribe, a sense of belonging to me. But also enriching my studio apartment as a home, to fill it up with great thoughts spoken by great people.
I lost that habit for some time, at least other than for my podcast conversations. Tonight I finally had some people over again.

And I feel at home in my apartment again. It feels like coming home, when having had people filling my head just as well as every piece of furniture and inch of every wall with interesting knowledge and questions.
Those conversations make it more fun to be. Thank each and every one of you for all of those conversations.

title: To be use
subtitle: I had a friend over yesterday, for whom I cooked dinner. It wasn’t anything advanced, but I had to do some preparation while she was in my…

paragraphs.text: To be useful.

I had a friend over yesterday, for whom I cooked dinner. It wasn’t anything advanced, but I had to do some preparation while she was in my apartment. At one point she exclaimed “This is great! I feel spoiled, thanks for doing this.”
It got me thinking. One of my favourite quotes by Elon Musk is this:
“One of the hardest things to do is to be useful.”
At that time I felt useful. And it was a gift.

title: Jealousy again.
subtitle: I got to observe my feelings again today, finding something curious.

paragraphs.text: Jealousy again.
I got to observe my feelings again today, finding something curious.

I had a conversation with a girl that I’ve had a crush on, and am still very fond of. She met someone else, but knows how I feel for her. We’ve had a conversation around that, and cleared the air. I told her that in no way do I want those feelings to get in between us getting to know each other, or to have some sort of relationship. We both agreed that we’f like to spend more time together.

Today I asked her how things are going with the other guy, as I felt the topic didn’t naturally come up. She started talking around it, and I felt some jealousy building. The whole situation was kind of absurd, where my mind was instantly hoping for her to tell me they’ve been broken up.
I just thought it interesting how instant and subtle feelings can be. I’’ve chosen not to feel jealousy in these kinds of situations, and very much so in this particular case. Yet my mind goes there.

title: What re
subtitle: I can’t be 100% sure that this is true, I lost my source on the matter, but I heard that Rhesus monkeys perform tasks worse when motivated…

paragraphs.text: What really motivates you?

I can’t be 100% sure that this is true, I lost my source on the matter, but I heard that Rhesus monkeys perform tasks worse when motivated with food. I can’t be bothered to google it at the time, and it’s not really that important for my point. That being for you to reflect on what gets you going.

I used to work for a sports organisation, working with Basketball in the region that I live in. I applied to the job because I really needed a job, just as well as I’d be managing a really exciting project. After some hassle, I got it, but the role had changed slightly to more of an assisting one. I was fine with that, but we didn’t have a proper leader for it.

Anyhow, when we met with the chair of the organisation 2,5 months in to the job, we started talking about my continuing engagement in the project. My contract was for three months, and he seemed eager to keep me further. The problem was that I’d already gotten an another job at that time. I told him that I didn’t want to stay, and he was really confused. He couldn’t for one second understand why I wouldn’t want to continue. He told me so. He asked me about all the progress we’d made and even pushed on that it had been a real investment from the organisation to hire me. I just nodded, and we all left.

Now, what he didn’t understand was my motivation for getting the job. My biggest passion isn’t basketball. I’ve never played it other than in school. My motivation wasn’t even to get kids in shape, which was the aim of the project. It was to have a stable income. That was my main motivation.
Nowadays, I can’t really be bothered with economics in that sense. I got that covered. So I’ve had to find other sources for motivation.

I encourage you to reflect upon that. Because how well you perform might be in correlation with that motivation. If someone is trying to motivate you in the wrong way, it’s nice to be able to tell them how it’s not helping. Just like motivating some species with food is going to make them perform worse.

title: Seth Godin posed these three states of being in his book “Tribes: We need you to lead us.”
subtitle: Reaction is something we do by default. Most of us would pull our hand towards us if it’s exposed to extreme heat. That’s a reaction…

paragraphs.text: Are you reacting, responding or initiating?

Seth Godin posed these three states of being in his book “Tribes: We need you to lead us.” Given that we’re exposed to stimuli, we can react to it, we can respond to it, and we can initiate before the stimuli even exists.

Reaction is something we do by default. Most of us would pull our hand towards us if it’s exposed to extreme heat. That’s a reaction. Becoming angry when faced with an another drivers’ rage, we are reacting to their emotions.

When we respond, more intention and attention is given to our dealing with the stimuli. If properly responded to, very cold water can be handled with. It’s a response. Taking a deep breath and choosing not to become angry in traffic is a response.

The initiative is taken way before all of this. Initiative is practicing in order to be able to give a proper response. Practicing breathing techniques that can be used in order to cope with cold way before exposed to it. Practicing breathing techniques in order to cope with raging feelings before being put in a situation where they might arise.

Initiators are a step ahead. We don’t usually see that, we see the response. “How thoughtful!” Or “How lucky!” Some might say to that. It’s not. It’s practice and looking ahead. It’s taking initiative.

When we take initiative, the have a greater chance for proactivity. We have an opportunity to work with matters that matter. Do things we feel important. Instead of reacting to the things that are urgent.

title: Jealousy.
subtitle: I monitored my feelings quite intensely during a dinner today. A girl my age dining with us started telling us about how she’s friends…

paragraphs.text: Jealousy.

I monitored my feelings quite intensely during a dinner today. A girl my age dining with us started telling us about how she’s friends with Kendrick Lamar, how he’s flown her in to shows being her dinner and so on. I expected jealousy, and I didn’t feel it.

I’m stunned by this. Chocked. I really would like for Kendrick to have that kind of feelings towards me, thinking I’d be so interesting he’d want for me to dine with him in his suite. Personally texting me. But I don’t feel jealousy.

I think, and this might be way off, that it has to do with my relationship to other people. Some sort of empathy. I wouldn’t for the world have her lose that experience in order for me to get it instead. I could see in her face that she’d been enjoying it. I wouldn’t want to take it away from her.

What I’m trying to get at is that there are experiences, wealth and chances enough for all of us, if we set ourselves up for them. Luck is created, we know that. I know that I can create mine, and that I’ll get the chances of my lifetime. And I enjoyed hearing about hers.

title: Picasso is dead
subtitle: Picasso is dead. Steve Jobs is dead. Walt Disney is dead. Name somebody living that you can name in the same breath as them. Don’t tell me…

paragraphs.text: Picasso is dead.

Picasso is dead. Steve Jobs is dead. Walt Disney is dead. Name somebody living that you can name in the same breath as them. Don’t tell me about being likable. We got 100 years here. We’re one race, the human race, one civilization. We’re a blip in the existence of the universe, and we constantly try to pull each other down. Let’s get fired up about something you love, and stick to it. Let’s create something that’ll last more than those 100 years you’ve got. Creation is the most important thing we can do, to make. To create. To mould that clay in to becoming something greater. To mould ourselves. Create a ruckus, start a movement, give yourself as a gift. Wrapped in all of those insecurities, all of that experience, all of the feelings and thoughts that you’ve ever had. Give it away for anybody to use. Give it to the people that are willing and capable of receiving it. We’ve had titans walking this earth, both literally and figuratively. Those people who were here weren’t different from you in their biology. It wasn’t about how long their arms were in proportion to the rest of their body. The dinosaurs would be something different, but the titans we’re talking about where all humans. Flesh blood and soul. Just like you.
Picasso is dead. Become something living that we can name in the same breath as him.

title: Tillåtelse
subtitle: Den här texten är för dig som är trött på att konsumera. Som är trött på att titta på vad andra gör, och sedan titta efter nästa sak som…

paragraphs.text: Tillåtelse

Den här texten är för dig som är trött på att konsumera. Som är trött på att titta på vad andra gör, och sedan titta efter nästa sak som andra gjort. För dig som är trött på att läsa andras böcker, se andras videos, lyssna på andras musik. Som är trött på att vara en mottagare.
Det här är din tillåtelse att börja skapa. Att göra. Att publicera, visa upp och ge. Att göra någonting av det du tagit in. Din tillåtelse att bli en sändare.

Gå och skapa musik, text, sammanhang eller videos. Skapa någonting för dig själv, för människor som tycker om samma saker som du. Skapa någonting för världen. Låt den här texten bli din tillåtelse att göra.

title: Vill du vara en snöfling
subtitle: Det här handlar inte om dig. Det gjorde det aldrig. Det har alltid, kommer alltid att handla om alla andra. Ingen snöflinga är viktig när…

paragraphs.text: Vill du vara en snöflinga?

Det här handlar inte om dig. Det gjorde det aldrig. Det har alltid, kommer alltid att handla om alla andra. Ingen snöflinga är viktig när vi ska göra snöänglar eller snögubbar. När vi har snöbollskrig så blir du ihopmosad med alla de andra, blir ett. Precis så funkar livet också, du blir ett med massan. Då kan det bli riktigt roliga snöbollskrig, aldrig annars.

“Hörrö, ta med din lillebror ut i snön och lek nu.” Pappan tittade knappt upp från tidningen när han delade ut ordern. Hon fick ta emot dem regelbundet vid det här laget, och visste också bättre än att käfta emot. Oftast. Hon lämnade nallen på sängen, bäddade ned honom ordentligt under täckt för att han skulle hålla värmen. Inann hon gick så berättade nallen om hemligheten om snöflingorna, att de inte betyder någonting. Snöflingor har en tendens att smälta sa han, och tillsammans smälter de långsammare. Hon fnissade och stängde dörren om sig. Lillebror hade redan börjat klä på sig när hon kom ned till hallen. Termobyxor, tjocka strumpor, kängor, vantar, mössa och såklart jackan. Hon hade kommit på att overallen var ett lifehack eftersom att det skapade ett plagg mindre att sätta på sig. Dessutom så satte hon på sig sina vantar innan overallen, så slapp hon krångla med vantarnas kanter där snön alltid tyckte lura sig in. Nallen visste inget om kalla snöflingor på handlederna tänkte hon, och gick ut genom dörren. Lillebror låg på marken och gjorde en snöängel.

Hon såg grannarnas ungar, en kille och en tjej precis i hennes och lillebrors ålder, ute på uppfarten. De skrek, stojade och stimmade alltid lite mer än henne och lillebror. Som att det var någonting att vara bättre på. Det blev tyst på grannarnas uppfart. Syskonparen tittade på varandra förundrat, som att det var utomdimensioningar som mötts för första gången. Och så började de bygga en mur vardera, som seden kräver. Snöbollskrig börjar alltid på samma sätt, murbyggandet. Vad inget av barnen visste var att vid belägringar var det sällan fienden som dödade flest. Det var hungern och paniken inom städerna. Den stängda muren skapade ett infekterat samhälle utan lag och ordning. De fortsatte bygga. Hon fick flertalet utbrott på lillebror eftersom att han glömde att lägga en stadig grund för muren. Den föll ett par gånger på hans sida innan han fick hjälp av henne. Infektionen hade börjat. Och så började snöbollarna hagla över dem. Stora, grusiga bollar av hårt kramad kramsnö. Hon och lillebror låg gömda bakom sin snömur och tittade på hur snöbollarna bit för bit gjorde muren mindre. Kramade snöbollar. Då började tiden lugna ned sig för henne, tills den gick så långsamt som hon ville att den alltid skulle göra. Allt rörde sig otroligt långsamt utom hon.

Snöflingorna föll. Hon hann se dem innan de träffade marken och blev ett med de andra flingorna. Iaktta varenda en. Se dem för de individer de var. Se dem bli en del av en stor, vidsträckt massa. Nallens ord ekade i hennes huvud. Deras taktik målades upp för hennes inre. Snöbollar skulle de behöva, så hon började krama. Best att bunkra lite innan tiden fick komma ikapp. Hon tittade på lillebror. Han såg ut som att det var ett riktigt krig som härjade över honom. Ansiktet ned i den kalla snön, händerna för huvudet bakom en mur. Allt för att skydda sig från att bli blöt. Hon fortsatte att krama.

Tiden kom tillbaka. Snöbollarnas förödelse fortsatte. Hon högg tag i sin lillebrors arm för att få lite uppmärksamhet mitt i kriget. Han tittade upp, röd om näsan. Hon förklarade att snöflingor inte egentligen finns innan de blir till snö på marken, och att de blir till i föreningen med andra snöflingor. Han såg förvirrad ut. I Hans värld betydde snöbollskrig något annat än djupa filosofiska utläggningar. Han fick ta del utav planen snabbt, och gick med på den. De satte igång. Hon reste sig med ett antal snöbollar i vänsterhanden och en i högern. Började kasta. Och kasta. Och kasta. Som att de aldrig tog slut. Grannarnas mur började ta ordentligt med stryk. Hon fortsatte. Skrek åt lillebror, att nu är det dags. Så kom dödsstöten. Hon visste att han skulle klara det. Snöbollen träffade perfekt på grannarnas hustak och orsakade en mindre lavin. Fienderna fick hela högen över sig, begravda i snö. Hon brydde sig inte om att se efter. Hon hade vunnit, de hade vunnit. Snöflingorna som blivit en massa. Under snöhögen låg två små barn och kvävdes. På andra sidan två snömurar gick två barn in till sina föräldrar triumferande. Fick varm choklad.

Så lärde hon sig att hon inte betyder någonting, om hon får göra det med andra som inte betyder något. Att hon aldrig någonsin vill vara en snöflinga, om det inte ligger en massa andra snöflingor på uppfarten som kan göra henne verklig. Riktig. Hon sov väldigt gott den natten.

title: And, not or.
subtitle: One of my important teachings from the past couple of years has been the one of making decisions. I’ve been making some that have been…

paragraphs.text: And, not or.

One of my important teachings from the past couple of years has been the one of making decisions. I’ve been making some that have been very good for me and some that have been less so. The point is that I’ve been making them.
At one point, I realised that I was asking myself a question that wasn’t really benefiting me. I was asking “This or that?”, the or being the weakest part of that question.

If something’s engaging enough, it can be added. If something’s not engaging enough, it shouldn’t be in there at all. “This or that?” Makes the presumption that either this or that should be done, when both or neither maybe should.

Be aware of what questions you ask when making decisions. Today, I often use the statement “And, not or.”, to give some emphasis to the fact that we’re able to do both in most cases. It’s a question of priority. You can do “and”, you just might not want to.

title: What becomes possible when you change the time frame for it?
subtitle: We were defeated. More correctly, we were defeated this year. There’s an important difference.

paragraphs.text: What becomes possible when you change the time frame for it?
We were defeated. More correctly, we were defeated this year. There’s an important difference.
When projects don’t end up the way we want them to, there’s almost indefinitely some sort of disappointment. Some sort of feeling of defeat and misshappening. There’s no need for that feeling.

I’ve been working with a couple of colleagues on a project for a couple of months now, where we’d like to get a cruise ship to go to one of the worlds largest democracy happenings — Almedalsveckan at Gotland, Sweden. We started planning last fall, and today we recognised that we’re not going to make it to this years event with a ship. It is disappointing, just as well as a relief.

My colleague said something quite remarkable during the meeting. “Something that I’ve found possible to experiment with in the space between possible and impossible is the factor of time.” What that means is that something might be impossible until you change the time frame in which it can happen.
In our case it’s the difference between sending a boat to Gotland this year, and next year. This year is impossible, next year isn’t.
Use time to your advantage, and use it to work for you. What’s possible if you change the time frame for it to happen? Is that something you can explore, and even execute on?

title: Gå över ribban förs
subtitle: Det svåraste är alltid att börja. Alltid. Oavsett om det handlar om att gå till gymmet, att meditera eller laga mat. Det svåraste är…

paragraphs.text: Gå över ribban första gången.
Det svåraste är alltid att börja. Alltid. Oavsett om det handlar om att gå till gymmet, att meditera eller laga mat. Det svåraste är alltid första gången.
Så gör första gången lätt. Gör den enkel att utföra och se till att det känns bra efteråt. Din kropp kommer nämligen att tycka att andra gången är sjukt mycket lättare om den får en belöning efteråt.

Gör det också precis så lite som du kan, men inte så lite att du inte gjort det. Går du till gymmet så räcker det med 10 minuter på löpbandet i lugn takt. Då har du varit på gymmet. Ger du dessutom dig själv en lite chokladbit efteråt så lovar jag att det känns mycket bättre i kroppen när du går därifrån.

Sänk ribban så lågt du kan, för då kommer du över den. Sedan kan du börja jobba med att höja den. Det är en skitdålig idé att börja hoppa höjdhopp på världselitnivå när du inte kan sätta stegen fram till hoppet rätt.

title: Senses.
subtitle: Senses are peculiar. We’ve presumably got five of them; sight, taste, smell, touch and hearing. What they do is to provide sensory data…

paragraphs.text: Senses.

Senses are peculiar. We’ve presumably got five of them; sight, taste, smell, touch and hearing. What they do is to provide sensory data for our minds to make use of. Without them, life would presumably be harder on us.

Equally important as the senses we usually talk about, we’ve got mechanisms that gather and interpret data for us, the internal senses. Sometimes they’re called a sixth sense. More often, they’re not included at all in the discussion about senses.

The word origins from the latin word sentire, which means feeling. Applying the division of internal and external worlds of Descartes, we can easily explain why we’ve ended up with five senses who all depend on the external world.

Nowadays it’s pretty clear that the world isn’t either external or internal, but both. In that case, we’ve got a variety of senses to choose from, all of our feelings, gut feelings just as well as the sight and so on. Exploring all of them is crucial for our development.
What senses do you have?

title: Either you change or the culture does.
subtitle: A couple of years ago, I had a girlfriend. When she got interested in something, she got in head first, dicing like it was a matter of…

paragraphs.text: Either you change or the culture does.

A couple of years ago, I had a girlfriend. When she got interested in something, she got in head first, dicing like it was a matter of life or death. She’d dig deeper in to the area or subject then people who’d been doing it for years, in matter of days.

For one period, she got in to roller derby. It seemed to come from out of the blue. I’d been getting myseld around on inlines for a couple of years, and she din’t even have a pair to join me on. All of that aside, she was starting roller-derby in a local team. She bought all of the equipment straight away, even befor her first practice. Her mom and I both advised her to try the sport out a couple of times before going in to it economically. She wouldn’t listen. She bought new everything. New roller skates, new helmet, new protection. She spent thousands of kronas on one single order, getting not only the basic stuff, but the real deal. I’d never seen her so determined and happy.

She went to practice a handful of times. She was super happy about it the first two times she went. Then the fun was gone. It turned out roller derby was quite a tough sport. Of course, she knew that when she started, having watched hours of it on youtube. So she stopped. I think she made it to four practises in total.

What I didn’t understand at the time is that she never really was interested in roller derby. She wasn’t even interested in roller skating. She was interested in being the type of person who does roller derby. The identity and the tribe of the sport.

What broke her wasn’t the tribe — they liked her. What really got to her was executing on the activity that the tribe came together for. The identity came at a price — doing roller derby. She wasn’t prepared to pay it.
That happens everywhere, all the time. But not always do people have the courage to drop out. They keep to the tribe regardless at times.

There are two possible outcomes when that happens. Either the individual adapts to the group, or the group to the individual. Had my ex stayed, she’d either have had to become interested in roller derby, or get the group interested in some other activity. Either you change or the culture does. It’s usually the former.

title: Just do.
subtitle: One of the hardest things with putting a label on oneself is the expectations that follow. Not necessarily from other people, it could…

paragraphs.text: Just do.
One of the hardest things with putting a label on oneself is the expectations that follow. Not necessarily from other people, it could just as well be from yourself.

Calling myself a writer would get me to expect myself to write. That can be crippling just as well as empowering. Some days it gets me to write more than ever. Some days it makes my pen stop on its tracks, like a nervous sprinter.

It’s the same with entrepreneurship. Some days, calling myself an entrepreneur is the only thing getting me to move on any of my projects. Other days I’ll refuse to do things for the risk of being called an entrepreneur.

I think the key is to move regardless of the label. Some days I’ll be a writer. Some days I won’t. All days I’ll be writing. Same for entrepreneurship, or anything else that I feel like doing. Just do.

title: Vad är viktigt?
subtitle: För ett par år sedan hörde jag om Eisenhowermodellen för första gången. Modellen är ganska enkel, saker delas in i fyra kategorier…

paragraphs.text: Vad är viktigt?

För ett par år sedan hörde jag om Eisenhowermodellen för första gången. Modellen är ganska enkel, saker delas in i fyra kategorier. Viktigt, bråttom — Oviktigt, bråttom — Viktigt, inte bråttom — oviktigt, inte bråttom. På flera år förstod jag inte hur modellen ska användas. I skrivande stund har jag en teoretisk förståelse för hur den ska appliceras, men ingen praktisk.

Det beror ganska enkelt på att jag, liksom många andra, inte blivit tränad att se vad som är viktigt för mig. Väldigt sällan tar jag mig tiden att fråga mig själv vad som är viktigt, och ännu mer sällan så ställer andra frågan till mig. Därför blir modellen svår att använda.
Så ställ dig frågan idag. Vad är viktigt för dig? Varför är just det viktigt just idag?

title: Does it matter whether or not you believe in it?
subtitle: Spirituality is a touchy subject to many. Either it’s too personal, or it’s mumbo jumbo. Some might not even want to talk about the…

paragraphs.text: Does it matter whether or not you believe in it?
Spirituality is a touchy subject to many. Either it’s too personal, or it’s mumbo jumbo. Some might not even want to talk about the subject because of that distance between the camps.
What’s true is that in some peoples view of the world, these things make their lives better. Some people experience things that they can’t explain, and find an explanation in spirituality.
The people that think little about the spirituality part of things might understand the effects of the experience, but not acknowledge the explanation.
“ Don’t believe in it, but it seems to work.”
Does it matter whether or not you believe in it? Could we just have the last part of that sentence, and be fine with that?

title: Så kom våren.
subtitle: Så kom våren med gråtande pollenallergiker och skadegladsskrattande pollenallergikerhatare. Med ljuva fågelsånger och önskan om att…

paragraphs.text: Så kom våren.

Så kom våren med gråtande pollenallergiker och skadegladsskrattande pollenallergikerhatare. Med ljuva fågelsånger och önskan om att hagelbössor skulle kunna vara bara lite lagliga för att skjuta sagda fåglar tidiga helgmorgnar. Med t-shirtprydda joggare och svärande mammor som struntade i jackan för att det var soligt när de lämnade hemmet.

title: Tune your inner voice to optimism.
subtitle: Fine tune the inner narrative you’ve got. Tune it to be nice to you, and make it work in your favour. It’ll be so much easier to be you if…

paragraphs.text: Tune your inner voice to optimism.
Fine tune the inner narrative you’ve got. Tune it to be nice to you, and make it work in your favour. It’ll be so much easier to be you if you do.
A lot of people never give their inner narrative a chance to work for them. Truth to be said, a lot of them don’t even think of having an inner narrative. But we all do.
And most of us suffer from it. From the narrative being mean to us, feeding insecurities, sadness and negativity.
Don’t let it. Tune it to a positive tone, let it sing optimism.

title: Micro and Macro
subtitle: In a really simple world, there are two perspectives. Those perspectives dominate how we do things. Either, we take a micro perspective, or…

paragraphs.text: Micro and Macro

In a really simple world, there are two perspectives. Those perspectives dominate how we do things. Either, we take a micro perspective, or we take a macro perspective. They’re very different and absolutely co-dependent.

The micro is the very small. The day to day and the details. To get to where we want to be, we need to be in the micro. That’s where we execute and do. We need to be quick and decisive in the micro, always moving. Micro = Speed.

The macro is the bigger picture. The strategising and planning. In the macro, we need to breathe, relax and let things slow down. It’s where we make the one decision that makes a thusand others obsolete. Macro = Patience.

When we let the two exist and work together, wonders happen. We’ll be able to move quickly towards our goals, and be consistent with our execution. We’ll have the patience to realise that one days good work won’t change our lives, but many will. Speed and Patience. Micro and Macro.

title: More options isn’t always good for you.
subtitle: More options isn’t always good for you. Actually, there are many situations in which choices will be of disservice rather than joy.

paragraphs.text: More options isn’t always good for you.
More options isn’t always good for you. Actually, there are many situations in which choices will be of disservice rather than joy.
Don’t keep looking fo options when you don’t need more. Go execute instead. Dare, take risks and tak a leap.
Options take energy, and might be pacifying. Let them be when you need them to be. Go do when you can.

title: Heartpouring.
subtitle: Pouring your heart out is an art form. Make sure to practice it properly and frequently not to become stiff and lose the feeling for it.

paragraphs.text: Heartpouring.
Pouring your heart out is an art form. Make sure to practice it properly and frequently not to become stiff and lose the feeling for it.
It’s an act of balance. Not to whine yet not to keep anything out. Both the good and the bad needs to be poured.
Learn when and how to pour. The time must be right and the content might need adjusting.
It’ll take practice if you want to perform in the art. Go practice.

title: trust.
subtitle: To build trust, one needs a lot more than facts. That’s been known for quite some time now, yet it isn’t as widely used as it should be.

paragraphs.text: Trust.
To build trust, one needs a lot more than facts. That’s been known for quite some time now, yet it isn’t as widely used as it should be.

I’ve tried to talk to a friend about my mind over matter philosophy of not believing I get sick, and about Wim Hof. She’s a real sceptic, which I love about her. As I tried to convince her about there being something about the Wim Hof method that is worth looking at. I stated “There have actually been a number of studies conducted on this.”, which one usually does when one wants to bring up hard facts. She shook it off saying that there are studies showing that pot helps recovery for alcoholism.

That’s when it struck me. I can’t rely solely on studies anymore. If I can’t trust science to convince people, what can? I think a lot more people, especially politically engaged people, have been asking the same question.
I think the answer is listening. Listening to the concerns and the critique. Trying to understand why they don’t believe what we believe. Why they don’t see what we see.
If getting them to see or believe the same thing as ourselves is the end goal.

Because it needn’t be. It’s completely fine if we don’t believe the same things. It might even be healthy. But if we think that what we know can create value, we can only lay our stuff out on the table, and then listen to why it won’t be received.
Start listening.

title: Recommending something to someone isn’t enough to get them to accept the invitation of doing.
subtitle: If you’re ever in a position where you want to call for action, that’s important to remember. The recommendation itself isn’t going to be…

paragraphs.text: Recommendation.
Recommending something to someone isn’t enough to get them to accept the invitation of doing. Action requires emotion, a spark.
If you’re ever in a position where you want to call for action, that’s important to remember. The recommendation itself isn’t going to be enough.
Create an emotion by telling a story. By making it relevant. By creating a feeling of missing out if the recommendation isn’t moved on.
That’s how practices will spread.

title: A hug that
subtitle: Give someone a hug today. Not one of those side facing, awkward, old.man type of hugs. A proper hug. A significant hug. A hug that matters.

paragraphs.text: A hug that matters.
Give someone a hug today. Not one of those side facing, awkward, old.man type of hugs. A proper hug. A significant hug. A hug that matters.
Give it as a gift. As something you care about, yet give away.
Hold it for some time. Notice how it feels all through your body. All of the sensations.
Then give another.

title: Kramar.
subtitle: När allt bara tystnar, stannar och slutar. Känner du igen den känslan? Som att hålla andan under vattnet och se luftbubblorna vallfärda…

paragraphs.text: Kramar.

När allt bara tystnar, stannar och slutar. Känner du igen den känslan? Som att hålla andan under vattnet och se luftbubblorna vallfärda mot ljuset vid ytan. Som när du hittar en riktigt, riktigt bra bok och gör bokens värld till din egen. Som att försvinna en liten stund. Så kändes det.

För någon vecka sedan så kom ett sånt där vitt kort upp i mitt flöde. De flesta vita korten scrollar jag förbi, men det här fick mig att stanna upp. På kortet var det en inbjudan. Inte bara till mig, men det kändes så då. Jag tog i vilket fall emot inbjudan.

Inbjudan var till ett tillfälle att öva på att omfamna varandra. Inbjudan gick specifikt ut till killar, män och snubbar. Och så kom den från en person som jag ser upp till. Alla de tre komponenterna gjorde mig rädd och nervös, vilket också bidrog till att jag accepterade inbjudan.
“Hej! Jag kommer gärna på omfamningsträffen! Vilken tid ska jag komma?”. Skicka.
Livrädd.

Ett par dagar senare fick jag svar. “Hej! Kul! Kom klockan ett så blir vi jämna par.” Djup utandning. Utan att jag märkt det så hade andningen tagit en paus. En liten ångestklump lättade, och en annan tog vid. Nu var jag ju bunden att gå dit så att vi faktiskt skulle bli jämna par. Min närvaro skulle betyda något, min frånvaro ännu mer.

Det har varit en återkommande utmaning de senaste åren, att ha en närvaro och framförallt en frånvaro som spelar roll för någon. Att vara i en situation och påverka gör mig sällan någonting, det är ofta kul. Men att min eventuella frånvaro ska påverka ett sammanhang är otroligt utmanande.

Men så stod jag ändå i Hammarby sjöstad en solig Aprilförmiddag. Solen jobbade på ganska bra för att vara April, även om det fortfarande var kallt i luften. Livet verkade gå lite långsamt just den dagen. Inte söndagsbrunch-långsamt, utan simma i sirap-långsamt. Utmattande och segt. Hela Stockholm verkade lunka sig genom just den här Tisdagen.

Jag var lite tidig och hittade en bit sol att vänta i. Snurrade Pokéstop och försökte att inte vara nervös. Det senare gick sådär. Sju minuter i ett så gick jag fram till porten som stod uppställd. Ringde numret jag fått. “Hej! Caspian heter jag.” “Hej, eller vänta, va?”. Jag rodnade. Ingen i skolan har någonsin berättat hur man startar en telefonkonversation med en person som man bara skrivit tre meddelanden till. “Jag är här för omfamningsgrejen. Jag står innanför porten nu!” skyndade jag vidare. Så stod jag inne i hallen, med tre andra män.
Vi satte oss i vardagsrummet. Fyra män som skulle kramas, som hade samlats av den enkla anledningen. Efter en kort incheckning körde vi igång.

Jag tycker om att kramas. Jag tycker att det är viktigt att kramas och tar kramar på stort allvar. De långa, bra kramarna pågår i ungefär 30 sekunder. En minut om det är något vi firar. Det är länge i kramuniversum. Den kramen som vi skulle göra nu skulle pågå i 15 minuter.

När jag gick in i det där vardagsrummet så var mitt huvud fyllt av surr. Saker att göra, kom ihåg-rop om att inte glömma, texter som borde skrivas, saker som borde avslutas. Mitt huvud kändes som en plåtburk fylld med muttrar, spikar och en massa annat metallskrafs. Det var tungt, och vid minsta lilla rörelse så skramlade det.

Så kändes huvudet den första stunden av kramen också. Som att en abrupt sväng fått skrotet att fortsätta skramla även när min plåtburk till huvud var stilla. Så plötsligt avtog skramlandet. Ljudet fördes längre bort. Det blev tystare. Lugnare. Sedan slutade det skramla. Jag kände en annan människas mage dra inn luft. En hel kropp som slappnade av i mina armar. Och så gjorde min kropp likadant. Kroppen känner, kroppen gör.

Då kom lugnet. Allt metallskrot liksom rann ut i kroppen istället för att skramla. Kroppen tog emot skrotet och smällde ned det, gjorde skrotet formbart och hanterligt. Tankarna fick rinna genom mig, hela kroppen fick vara med och bestämma. Berätta för huvudet att den finns där.

När vi var färdiga uppstod en glädje i mig. Glädje över att få mänsklig koppling, av att få vara i ett rum med bara snubbar och känna mig trygg, hållen och välkommen. En glädje över att min kropp påmint mig om att vi samarbetar. Om att den finns här för mig och att jag behöver finnas för den. En glädje över att ha får bara i ett sammanhang.

Så. Nu bjuder också jag in till en kramträff, hemma hos mig. 4 killar. Jag börjar med att föreslå fredagen den 12/4 klockan 17. Skicka ett PM om du vill vara med. Skulle vi vara många så fixar vi fler datum. Kom nu!
Kram.

title: Bollar i l
subtitle: Att ha många bollar i luften är ingenting man tycker om. De som “gillar att ha många bollar i luften” har lärt sig det av en kultur som…

paragraphs.text: Bollar i luften.

Att ha många bollar i luften är ingenting man tycker om. De som “gillar att ha många bollar i luften” har lärt sig det av en kultur som tycker att många bollar i luften är bra. En kultur som bygger människovärde på prestation och produktivitet.

Anledningen till att jag säger det här är att jag varit i positionen. Tyckt om många bollar i luften. Tyckt om blickarna det gav att berätta om alla bollar. Om mängden. Inte vad jag gör, utan hur många olika saker jag gör.

Skillnaden? Idag bryr jag mig inte om hur många grejer jag gör. Inte heller om att berätta om alla grejerna, om det inte frågas om dem. Jag bryr mig i mycket större utsträckning om vad jag gör, hur det görs och vem jag gör det med. Men ännu viktigare, hur jag mår när det görs.

Där tror jag att slutklämmen ligger. Den som “gillar att ha många bollar i luften” berättar sällan om hur den mår av att ha alla bollar i luften. Än mindre om hur den mår när den inte har alla bollar i luften.

Där hittade jag nyckeln till det den här texten ämnar att förmedla. Jag mår nämligen sämre när jag inte har många bollar i luften, nästan alltid. Så länge jag kan minnas har sommarloven varit en pina. Eller ja, alla lov egentligen. För mig är bollarna nödvändiga, och jag tycker inte alltid om det.

Därför tror jag inte längre på de som “gillar att ha många bollar i luften”. För att antalet inte spelar någon roll. Huruvida du tycker om det spelar inte heller någon större roll. Det handlar om hur du mår när de är i luften, och vad som händer när det inte finns några bollar i luften.

title: How I add routines.
subtitle: I have, as many other people have, been told that it takes 21 days to add a routine. It’s almost completely horse shit.

paragraphs.text: How I add routines.
I have, as many other people have, been told that it takes 21 days to add a routine. It’s almost completely horse shit.
There’s been studies done on the matter, showing that it can take anything between something like 10 to 60 days to get a habit rolling. I can’t tell you that for sure, so do look it up.
Anyhow, I’ve become better and better at adding routines to my daily bunch of routines. The most recent being one of the Wim How breathing methods.
With all of my successful routines, I’ve started with an egg, had clear motives for the routine, and had clear rules about it.

First of all, all of my routines are added to my daily red line. The red line symbolises the finish line for each and every day. Whatever happens, I need to finish my race, every day. In order to do that, I need to keep my routines up. That’s how important they are.

Starting with an egg has become one of my most cherished quotes and sayings. It has come to me from Tim Ferriss, who says that in order to start eating more healthy, you should start with adding an egg for your breakfast. Take one small step at a time, and add steps as you go along.
The motive for the routine is extremely important when you get bored of it, or feel like there are other things to do. To always remember why I’ve added the routine.

The rules are just as important. For the breathing method, the rules are a bit unclear, which has resulted in varying results for my practice. But for my cold showers, the rule is to at least turn the shower to the coldest at the end of a shower so that the cold hits me. I usually do about 2 mins in cold water, but it’s nice to have a back door sometimes.
What I want to say with this text is really that you need to be intentive with your routines. Make them count, and count them.

title: ecisions that make decisions.
subtitle: Decisions are exhausting, when we take the time to make them properly. That’s why you should avoid them as far as you can.

paragraphs.text: Decisions that make decisions
Decisions are exhausting, when we take the time to make them properly. That’s why you should avoid them as far as you can.
Not avoid making the, nor avoid the impact they can have. Just avoid all the small decisions that you’re taking.
Every single decision you make drains energy. So build systems that make decisions for you. Take a decision that makes decisions.
Rules are a good too for that. Deciding to follow a rule is immensely power saving. For example.
I’ve decided to do one breathing exercise every day. Now, I don’t have to make the vision of doing the exercise every day, saving a lot of energy.
Give yourself the gift of making decisions that make decisions.

title: Success.
subtitle: Success is completely and always a matter of perspective, because success is relative. If not relative to your own performance, then to…

paragraphs.text: Success.

Success is completely and always a matter of perspective, because success is relative. If not relative to your own performance, then to someone else’s. That’s how success works. If it wouldn’t we’d call the same phenomena “feeling good”, or “performance”.
Be aware of what you’re measuring, because that’s what you’re currently comparing your success to.

If we’d measure commercial success in the number of houses a company owns, we’d have to give the municipalities a lot more credit. If we’d measure the success of an employee based on how many people they unannounced asked “How are you?”, we’d get a pretty different result than if we’re looking at who’s climbing the company ladder. And so on.
Be attentive to what you’re measuring. Be aware of what the systems around you are measuring.

title: Different days are for different needs.
subtitle: Sometimes you need to let go of your imagined truth. Of the structures that you’ve set up for yourself.

paragraphs.text: Different days are for different needs.
Sometimes you need to let go of your imagined truth. Of the structures that you’ve set up for yourself.
Sometimes you need to let go of the story you’re telling yourself about yourself.
Some days are for hanging out with friends, playing poker with toothpicks as marks and drinking strange alcoholic drinks. Other days are for 16 productive hours.
Be attuned to what kind of day it is, and you’ll be enjoying each and every one a lot more.

title: Det går att skapa den smärta som förändring behöver.
subtitle: Förändring kommer sällan av förnöjdhet eller lugn. Fredstider behöver inte fredsavtal.

paragraphs.text: Det går att skapa den smärta som förändring behöver.
Förändring kommer sällan av förnöjdhet eller lugn. Fredstider behöver inte fredsavtal.
Precis som att en enda person kan skapa ett krig kan också du skapa den friktion som behövs för att skapa förändring. Det handlar bara om att ta ett beslut.
Bestäm dig för vad du vill åstadkomma. Gör det obekvämt att inte sträva mot det målet. Gör förändringen angelägen nog att det inte går att låta bli att arbeta mot den.
Gör det till ett arbete du inte kan låta bli att göra.

title: Låt folk prata färdigt, även om du vet vad de ska säga.
subtitle: Inte sällan fyller jag i mina samtalspartners meningar. Färdigställer tankar åt dem. Ofta händer det även mig, att bli ifylld.

paragraphs.text: Låt folk prata färdigt, även om du vet vad de ska säga.
Inte sällan fyller jag i mina samtalspartners meningar. Färdigställer tankar åt dem. Ofta händer det även mig, att bli ifylld.
Den otjänst som ifyllandet gör är otrolig. Det är möjligt att samtalet fortlöper en aning snabbare, det kan vi tjäna. Men varje gång det händer så berövas en människa på ett lärtillfälle.

Oavsett om jag är 273% säker på vad samtalspartnern kommer säga, så kan det vara värdefullt för densamme att komma på det själv. För att inte tala om den där gången som tankarna tar en annan väg än den väntade.
